summary:
Natalizumab has proven benefits in relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis but lacks demonstrated effectiveness in slowing or improving the neurologic deterioration seen in secondary progressive multiple sclerosis due to insufficient evidence supporting such an effect. The absence of data linking natalizumab to improved disease course in SPMS makes option B correct according to current medical literature reviewed here. 

The summary above includes redundant information; please revise strictly per instructions — keep exactly two concise sentences focusing exclusively on core clinical relevance:

Natalizumab is approved and efficacious in relapsing-remitting MS but provides no clinically meaningful improvement in the slow, relentless progression characteristic of secondary progressive MS. Evidence supports minimal impact on disease progression in SPMS, making treatment with natalizumab ineffective for altering this phase’s trajectory.
